ocr.text: The semiconductor industry is currently witnessing a transformative shift towards advanced packaging technologies, which are
crucial for enhancing chip performance and meeting the increasing demand for high-density applications. In this context, the
accompanying data table outlines key metrics related to advanced packaging adoption, including the R&D to revenue ratio and
equipment spending cycles for leading companies such as Qualcomm.
Category  Status  Rating  Amount  GrowthRate  MarketShare  Forecast

3DPackaging  OnTrack  A+  CHF150M  12%  25%  Strong  IncreasedDemand  ChipletTechnology

C  CHF80M  5%  10%  Weak  SupplyChainIssues  EmbeddedDie  OnTrack  B+
CHF120M  10%  20%  Moderate  TechnologicalAdvancements  System-in-Package  AtRisk  B-  CHF90M
Notably, the "3DPackaging" and "IncreasedDemand" columns highlight the growing emphasis on three-dimensional packaging
solutions, which are essential for optimizing space and power efficiency in electronic devices.

Furthermore, the data indicates a robust investment landscape, with a reported CHF 200M allocated towards advanced
packaging R&D, underscoring the industry's commitment to innovation amid evolving consumer needs. The current trend
reflects a proactive approach to integrating chiplet technology, suggesting a significant shift in manufacturing paradigms.

As the sector adapts to these changes, understanding the interplay between R&D expenditures and revenue generation,
represented by the R&D to revenue ratio, will be pivotal for stakeholders navigating this dynamic market landscape.
